tweets.text:
My little startup went from 0 to $4M ARR in 14 months.

We are going to $12M by EOY.

And we are about to make our most important hires:

2 founding engineers.

Up until now, we’ve had one absolute tank of a CTO building everything solo.

Now we are looking for his counterpart:
- https://t.co/nYE771neDC

U could be a multi-millionaire at 24 and it won't matter to the wrong people.

A bad room will downplay ur wins, question ur process, make u feel like a fraud.

Choose ur people like ur life depends on it.

Cause it prolly does.

Fastest way to lose confidence as a founder:

Stop doing what u said u would do.

“I’ll launch 10 creatives today” → u ship 3
“I’ll test that offer” → u push it a week
“I’ll fix the funnel” → never happens

Do that for 30 days straight and now u don't trust urself to execute.

x.name: Matthew Bertulli
x.bio: Founded 4 companies. 1 great exit -- Co-Host of Operators Podcast -- Just trying to be helpful.

tweets.text:
Are there ANY saas companies who run a really strong performance marketing machine?  

Thinking multi-channel ads here.

Who does this really well?

What he said.

I see too many founders optimizing towards top line growth "SCALE BRO!!" instead of maximizing free cash into their pockets.

Over the last 8 years I've made almost as much money from my investments compounding than I did from my first 2 exits.

If the average person can use AI to get their work done in half the time. 

Do they use the rest to do more work?

Or

Do they just take more time for other things?

Outside of entrepreneurs, I think most people choose to take the free time.
